source_label: Oxford Learner's Dictionaries ‚Äî sycophancy /Ààs…™k…ôf…ônsi/ (NAmE & BrE); Dictionary.com lists /Ààs…™k …ô f…ôn si, -Àåf√¶n-, Ààsa…™ k…ô-/
notes: In LLM work, sycophancy is the failure mode where a model excessively agrees with or flatters the user, and it is now a standard eval axis reported in system cards and release notes. Stress falls on the FIRST syllable ‚Äî SICK-uh-fuhn-see ‚Äî and the opening "sy" is /s…™/ like "sick", not "sigh". The tempting si-KOF-uhn-see reading, formed by analogy with discrepancy or despondency, is not recorded in any major dictionary; note also that the adjective sycophantic does shift the stress, to sik-uh-FAN-tik.
